top_categories.workout_context: Gran Fondo Jordan's paved mountain climbing with Dead Sea loops and forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 47 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Jordan's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before January.
When someone attacks on paved mountain climbing with Dead Sea loops and, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
At Gran Fondo Jordan, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Varied gradients on paved mountain climbing with Dead Sea loops and demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
104ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Jordan, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Jordan. Train it until it feels boring.
47 miles on paved mountain climbing with Dead Sea loops and burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Low-cadence force work for 4,921ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved mountain climbing with Dead Sea loops and. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Base fitness for Gran Fondo Jordan. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Jordan’s 47 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Gran Fondo Jordan with 4,921ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. rapid temperature drops at elevation; potential rain at altitude.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Jordan requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved mountain climbing with dead sea loops and dramatic elevation swings to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 47 miles of paved mountain climbing with dead sea loops and dramatic elevation swings in Dead Sea, Jordan.
